StatementVariantRef.description:
A problem-statement `extends` target referenced by (language, variant).

A bare string `extends: en` is shorthand for `{language: en}` with the
default variant (design §5).
StatementVariantRef.title: StatementVariantRef

description:
A problem-level statement. Identified by (language, variant) — it has no
`name` (design §3.1).

variant.description: Optional discriminator between formats of the same language. Together with `language` it forms the join key with contest statements.
variant.title: Variant

title.description: Title as it appears in the statement. Can be left unset to fall back to the package/contest title.
title.title: Title

file.description: Path to the input statement file. Required unless this statement `extends` another one to inherit its file.
file.title: File

type.description: Type of the input statement file.

params.description: This statement's own parameters, exposed to the template as the `params` namespace (kept separate from problem/contest `vars`).

samples.description: Whether to build the statement with samples.
samples.title: Samples

assets.description: Globs (relative to the package root) selecting files to ship as statement resources (e.g. images/PDFs). Inherited via `extends`. At build time the default image/PDF globs over the statement subtree and each sample subtree are concatenated to this list.
assets.title: Assets

extends.description: Another problem statement to inherit the build recipe from, referenced by language (`extends: en`) or by `{language, variant}`.
extends.title: Extends

title: Statement
